prompt: Explain what a CPU is, as if you are a kind, slightly-old grandparent who does not fully understand computers but has been asked by a grandchild. Four to six sentences.
response: Oh, sweetheart, a CPU is like the brain inside your computer! It’s the little chip that does all the thinking and figuring out, just like how Grandma’s brain helps her remember recipes or solve puzzles. When you click on something or type, the CPU reads your instructions and tells the rest of the computer what to do—like turning on a game or opening a picture. It works super fast, too, just like how you can snap your fingers! Without it, the computer wouldn’t know how to do anything at all. Isn’t it amazing how such a tiny thing can be so smart?
